reviews.review_text: I will not leave a positive review for this game, as you can read about how great the game is from many others. I will say I love this game and give ALL credit and praise to Harebrained Schemes for this wonderful masterpiece. I will say I HATE Paradox and HATE that they ruined HBS. For those that would like to try this game, NOTE that your money does not go to support the original development team. It goes to the greedy predators that is Paradox. Paradox also gutted the OG team of HBS, due to their demand of HBS to work on another failed title and not focus on what HBS already "knocked out of the park" with their other great titles. I'm grateful that my purchase of this game and the DLC's went directly to HBS. DO NOT support Paradox. Ugh, I hate Paradox.

The vanilla game is good to begin with, but it is the mods that make this game shine, BattleTech Advanced (Universe/3062) is the best of them,  

The tragedy is we won't get a sequel.

I really like Mechwarrior, the universe, the tabletop and the older action games. Battletech scratches the itch for tactical Mechwarrior gaming but there are quite some shortcomings.

The biggest shortcoming is, that there is there no tonnage restriction. It mostly makes no sense to field lower tonnage mechs this way. 

Additionally since ~35 hours into the game I was constantly fighting against lance[b]s[/b] including assault mechs with a total tonnage of ~400-600 tons with my ~ 250 tons. I bought any fitting heavy part I could and aimed to de-limb any interesting mech I wanted to, still my heaviest mech was an Archer. This only (mostly) worked since the AI is terrible, still, even with good loadouts and positioning you sometimes just nibble away the armour of one heavy/assault after the other and it's not thrilling, it's rather boring. 

Since I'm not into save scumming mechs got destroyed, pilots died and progress crawled down to a halt. (Edit: I save scummed and headshotted a Highlander and an Atlas, afterwards game got rather easy)

I'm not done with the game yet but am really close to throw in the towel. (Edit: I now rather enjoy the game again, changed to positive)

Do you like mechs?  Like stomping stuff?  Like turn based strategy?  Then this game is right for you!  A decent story in a fairly unused part of the Inner Sphere, the game's story will make you feel something at least.  But as with anything Battletech what you come for are the mechs and this game has it in spades!
Played this years ago and while it has it's quirks and shortcomings, it's still an excellent and replayable game.

Bought this many years ago. Finally beat the game recently. Loved the story, loved the tactical combat, love the Mechwarrior/Battletech universe. Sad we'll never see a part two of this due to licensing. It was a pleasure fighting as Mechwarrior.
If you want a gritty strategic game with Rich lore and a well written plot then this needs to be in your library.

xcom but mechs. mid voice acting and story. fun but repetitive gameplay. more mission variety, larger number of mechs fielded, battles with more than 2-3 factions present would be fun addition (not that 3 way fights have happened often anyways). After story is done, not much to do imo. havent bought DLC cuz they are over priced, but will see about getting them on sale. low replay value atm. Def buy the game on sale, its worth playing thru at least once. I think i got it for ~$10 and have logged 80 hours, so good deal.

Bought this game five years ago and just now completing the campaign. The learning curve is steep but once things clicked with mech customization, team composition, and ship economy, the game becomes really rewarding to play. The campaign story wasn't half bad and the battle scenarios they create for the campaign missions are decent.
I'm loving get back into the world of battletech and a pretty solid game so far

A great game to give a go, even in 2026. The cinematics, music, and overall story is at the peak of what Paradox has to offer in my opinion. I love games such as Stellaris or Hearts of Iron. But it goes without saying that NO other Paradox game comes this close to delivering an emotional/actual storyline to see unfolding, since this game doesn't really focus on grand strategy alone, but has a campaign/storyline to offer. 

The game reminds me of the "Ace Combat" series a bit. I wish we had more Paradox games with a story or a campaign to follow, I can only imagine how well they could deliver something like this now, especially with how good their cinematic DLC trailers have been lately for Stellaris.

Honestly a great, strategic game. It's a bit older now but still holds up well. If you liked XCOM or are a fan of the TTG that it's based on, I think you'll like this. Fun and interesting campaign, solid career mode options, 'mech customization, and a great mod community give BATTLETECH some long legs.
Fun it feels like X-Com mixed with Mechanicus. I have a feeling if you liked either game you will like Battletech.

this game was great. 
lots of fun trying to simply replay maps to get the right kills on the right mecs so that the right weapons are salvageable.
a game, within a game.

The tutorial and first few missions are kind rough. However once you get into your dropship and get access to the main map the game really opens up. You may have to spend some time learning things or google a mechanic or two as the tutorial leaves a few things out but otherwise it is a very solid game. Up there with the pinnacle that is the X-com games for me so far. Extra bonus if you're a Battletech fan.
This is a fantastic game. I continue to enjoy my time with it through the mods that are available because of my fellow fans. If you read this review give this game a chance.

I very good TBS, just short on story in my opinion. Very disappointed that hairbrained stopped supporting the game with content. 

give the game itself a 10/10 for mechanics and 5/10 for story and content. and Hairbrained gets a 0/10 for the lack of support for the brand. there should have been follow up games or multiple story packs by now.

Solid turn based start with lots of customization, decent mission variety (even if full enemy clears always win), cool story fluff, and a real table-top feel. Strong recommend if you're into warhammer or similar systems.

I have grown to like this game more the more I play it but the execution leaves much to be desired. The UI could use some work and the difficulty is too randomized to be reliable in some parts of the game.

Despite my complaints, there are a lot of things that I like about this game. I like that weapon tiers have impactful stat improvements like firing more times or reducing their weight. I like the different equipment options available early on for the arms, legs, torso, and the cockpit. In Mech Warrior 5 Mercenaries, arm and leg mods don't exist, and torso and cockpit mods are few and far between. I like the pilot perk and experience system. I think the story is quite nice, from what I experienced. Finally, I think the pilot events are a nice touch to add more flavor to your pilots, even if some of the choices have misleading results.

Out of Combat Gameplay Speed
Gameplay is mind numbingly slow, in combat and out. Out of combat, traveling jumps between systems individually, and when you travel a lot it gets quite annoying waiting for days to pass, for your ship to dock to a jump ship, for you to travel, and for the same jump animation to play for each system you pass through. It would be marginally better if you could at least shop at each system while you travel, but alas you cannot. Your game also pauses every time it thinks you get low on money, and it triggers after every planet you jump to. Considering that you cannot sell anything during travel, I find this warning amusing.

In-Combat Gameplay Speed
In combat, Battletech loves to absolutely swamp you with enemies, which means that turns can take ages even when you disable a lot of the pointless camera focuses and speed up the animations. Even with most of the camera features disabled, it still decides to arbitrarily zoom in and freeze in a random position, so you can miss combat outcomes. Most missions end up taking around half an hour or so in my experience. 

UI Complaints
There is also no indication in the turn order of which units go when. Yes, there is a turn order at the top of the UI, but you cannot see what units specifically go when; you can only infer. The MechWarrior skill tree doesn't let you preview the bonuses that subskills give if you are trying to specialize your pilots. You can't see the overview of an enemy mech's health unless the mech you are currently controlling can fire at that mech. If you ID a mech and then lose direct visual on it, Battletech suffers sudden onset amnesia and doesn't tell you what the mech was. These issues aren't game breaking, but they are kind of grating. 

Fog of War
One of my biggest annoyances still is the sort of arbitrary fog of war that units experience. Since enemies often run at you in waves, this can lead to their backline perpetually having line of sight against you while they are hidden in the fog of war. I am not saying that they are shooting at me from behind terrain, but that my pilots can only see mechs that are pressed up against their cockpit window, even if my mech would have line of sight or even if it was just shot at. They are often so far away that I do not even get the opportunity to sensor lock them because they aren't showing on my radar. Sometimes I experience units firing on me even when I don't think they have any line of sight and none of my units have been sensor locked. I want to believe this is because things you are supposed to destroy, like ammunition crates for example, technically count as enemies and are providing line of sight to other units. This only becomes frustrating when you are fighting several lances at once.

Swarms of Enemies
Having played further and gotten past the light mech hump, my biggest frustration is the randomness in contract difficulty. The mechs you face in contracts are randomized, to an extent. I have done the same two star mission and fought around 8 fifty ton mechs, reloaded and fought only 25 ton mechs. In most missions, the difficulty is fine because the number of mechs is manageable, but there are some missions where you are hit with two lances at a time, and in my experience that is where I have the most issues. For example, many missions will include a second reinforcement lance that usually comes in later rounds. Sometimes, though, they spawn practically next to each other meaning that you are now fighting two lances at once. You are not always told that you will be fighting multiple lances, so it can be frustrating when you are trying to do lower-tier missions because you don't have the firepower to take on multiple lances, only to be faced with multiple lances.

The gameplay is good, the mods make it even better.

The plot and story however is awful. Your character is sidelined by the 'princess' throughout the story, you have zero agency nor choice in the plot and must follow her every wim despite supposedly being an independend mercenary. The supporting characters are also awful. I can't believe HBS made a game with characters and story this bad.
It's a game though so get it on a sale for the gameplay and mod it for career mode.

Ultimately, this is a recommend (but in the year of our lawrd '26 or beyond, you should probably wait for a sale. They happen enough). But tbh it's probably an 8/10 game for me. Maybe even 7.5. You can tell the heart's there, but the game design is limited.

My big critique is that, regardless of mission structure, every one winds up playing out largely the same. Perhaps early game, you might play around more with light mechs on daring runs to pop vehicles before they get to the escape point, but by midgame, you have your formula. You know how to avoid or respond to ambushes (of which there are plenty; is there no military intelligence in this world?). As a result, replayability and expansions feel hollow. You can give me all kinds of new mechs, new maps, I will always kite the enemy lance into trickling into my killbox.

"But if it's not fun, try a different strat?" - Sure, you could, but willfully playing suboptimally feels its own flavor of bad. It doesn't help that there's almost zero incentive to drop anything less than your heaviest lance for every mission. Maybe late game, you might find a Heavy with unique capabilities that fits your style more than a bog standard assault. By then you'll have the parts to core even the heaviest mechs in two turns, at worst.

The game addresses this problem by just having you outgunned every mission, which doesn't make them more interesting, it just makes missions longer. It's "HP buffing" in tabletop format.

The Gordian Knot of this problem gets thicker because, as a result of the above, well-balanced mechs are frowned upon in favor of specialization/min-maxing your designs, resulting in odd frankenmechs that are often counter to their intended role. Brawlers become effective snipers, and snipers become effective brawlers (or sit in storage to be sold off because the game design tacitly frowns on true long-distance engagements). There are meta weapons in this game, and unfortunately in many cases, they aren't the fun ones. I would love to see some justice for Large Lasers, but it's 2026, that ship has sailed.

(Side note: It's just a little sad how heavy some weapons make the mechs, so like, you can't have an AC/2 shredder-sniper without all but running into combat naked. There is a meta to this game, and you will like it, even if it means "breaking" all your favorite toys to play the game).

But, like, it's there. The whole time I played this game, I felt the pulse of a good game underneath it all, just unfortunately strangled by the grip of balance changes, emergent meta, and what I assume are coding limitations or time crunch. A presume mods fix some of this, based on discussions online. I got my way through the campaign vanilla and enjoyed my time enough to walk away happy, and maybe someday I'll come back and try one or two mods out.

I've been voicing my grumps, so I do want to shine light on some high points: The OST is 10/10. No notes. It does everything I want and more. It's moody and epic in all the right places.

Other folks may disagree, but I liked the campaign plot, for what it was. Had I bought this game before twitter-rot set in, I might've even enjoyed going and talking to the various paper dolls scattered around the ship to learn their bits and bobs. Unfortunately I feel like the "downtime" aspect of the game is shortchanged by the game's construction (as compared to, say, Shadowrun, where being a part of the community is a major part of the game's "feel", I never really bonded with the ship's crew in the same way). I don't hold this against the writers, though -- what I read, I enjoyed. I just chose to not dig deep in the petty bs of the crew this time around.

Certainly, I think I prefer this plot to something like Homeworld 3's attempt at sci-fi space opera. But that bar is a tripping hazard.

So, yeah, it's "fun enough" (Jesus, I have 73 hours in this? When did that happen? Some of that must have been game running in the background, surely). But I wouldn't expect it to set the world on fire.
Love the game and it should be play vanilla before using mods.

Having just wrapped up the main campaign for the first time—with a massive 723 hours already clocked in-game—I can safely say I’ve been missing out for years.

As an augmented artist who writes a massive amount of music, an AAA ex-game dev (think survival genre), and a current indie dev working on a secret multimedia project, my daily life is a constant loop of bouncing audio and jumping between creative tasks. Because I am disabled and manage twice-exceptional ADHD (hence the deep-dive essay format, em dash use and AI structure for easy readability—I keep pace with the machine, not get it to do my job, just clean up my 2000+ mile-an-hour mind), I am usually running 7 to 8 things at once. When I'm not stalking targets or hunting headshots in DayZ, Arma Reforger, or The Forever Winter, I need a game that allows me to switch tasks without losing my creative momentum. BATTLETECH is exactly that vehicle.

I’ve known about the FASA lore, the 90s simulators, and the classic tabletop genesis since childhood—growing up with a brother heavy into D&D, while I was a massive sci-fi addict collecting Star Trek RPGs. Yet, I never dug deep into BATTLETECH or touched MechWarrior until now. What a massive oversight. This isn't an FPS or an RTS; it’s a beautifully executed Turn-Based Strategy (TBS) masterclass published by Paradox and crafted by Harebrained Schemes. Having played Stellaris extensively, I had my eye on this for a while, and it absolutely delivers.

What's Good

Faithful Mech Design & Lore: The depth and aesthetic execution of the BattleMechs are incredibly faithful to the source material, retaining a gritty, industrial weight.

Deep Combat Mechanics: There is an immense amount of mechanical meat to get your teeth into. Beyond armor vectors and heat positioning, there are layer-heavy systems like Electronic Warfare hidden deep in the mechanics for you to discover.

Solid Expansion Content: The DLCs offer excellent value, introducing a great variety of planet biomes and atmosphere types that actively change how your heat sinks function, alongside fresh chassis and novel tactical mechanics.

A Dope Soundtrack: As an audio guy, the soundscape and score hit all the right atmospheric notes to keep you locked completely in the zone. (Expect some electronic remixes down the line!)

Why It's a 9/10 (And Not a Perfect 10)

I’m notoriously hard to please, and after 700+ hours, a few minor friction points stand out:

Heavy Reading Requirements: To truly absorb the deep lore, you have to read a lot of text. When I’m busy or multi-tasking, it can get a bit overwhelming, and I find myself skipping sections. I wish more of these lore drops were fully voice-acted—though, to be fair to the developers, that is a massive financial investment, so go easy on them.

Endgame Scope: I lean toward massive scales, so I personally wish the star map was even larger with more robust endgame loops and massive deployment maps to push a veteran lance to its absolute limits. (Be aware, though, the community modding scene is huge; see the comments of this review for a brief breakdown).

Map Repetition: Some maps get reused a little too frequently during procedural contracts (or maybe I just don't like it when the terrain makes the drops too punishing!).

The Price to Value Ratio

Unlike games like MechWarrior Online, where the free-to-play model can easily hook you for £750+ if you want all the content packs, BATTLETECH treats your wallet with respect. I picked up the Digital Deluxe Edition on sale for £28.89 (it sits at £41.99 full price). Keep an eye on the Steam sales, because the sheer amount of tactical runtime you get for that price is an absolute steal.

Final Verdict: 9 / 10

Why the essay? As an ex/future game dev, I deeply value the architectural choices and polish that went into this project. The game is super fun, deeply relaxing, and incredibly accommodating for creators; I can just pick it up, put it down, and Alt-Tab back to work instantly. The developers deserve to be rewarded for this execution.

If you want a rewarding tactical strategy game that respects your pacing and gives you total mechanical control, don't pass this one up. It has easily earned its permanent spot in my studio rotation.



*see the comments for notes on the available mods as of June 2026—I hope the community finds this review useful* o7
Hands down the best battletech experience on PC. For fans and newcomers alike.

- Debt Simulator, can't buy any mech parts without hitting negative and salvage will nuke payout
- 1-2 skulls contract shouldn't be overdone with 1-2 heavy 'Mechs, mediums top
- 65%+ is somehow a massive miss for your lance, but for a hostile it's a hard hit
- Clans has better story telling than whatever this is

All of this within past two days because I been eyeballing the game.

I love BattleTech lore, but this game... It's trying to be BattleTech with something else.

[b]Support 32:9 resolution[/b]

I'm loving it. Still playing the campaign but I cant wait to play BTA 3062 mod so more options.

If your a fan of mechs and/or turn based combat, you owe it to yourself to try this.

I came in expecting MechWarrior: Mercenaries vibes. What I got was a part‑time job as a bankruptcy administrator.

Low mission payouts, repair times that feel longer than a Steiner family dinner, and AI that apparently graduated from the “I never miss” academy. Lose a mech? Lose a pilot? Congratulations, you’re now save‑scumming whether you like it or not.

And don’t get me started on the monthly bills. I swear my dropship is secretly subscribed to twelve streaming services.

One‑ and two‑skull missions should be warm‑ups, not “pray to RNGesus and hope your mech doesn’t explode” events.
Awesome Mech turn based combat with great mod support.
Excelente juego, ojalá hubieran mas así en la actualidad

clocked in 200+ hours 
Badly needs an expansion story or another DLC etc.
Its miniatures with all the effects and sounds you imagined in your head. What more could you want? Its awesome with some great mods out there too!
Love the action, the story, the leveling up process, the various mechs, weapons and scenarios. I hated the "should be easy" scenarios, because you always knew they would not be. Lovely game overall.

I keep coming back to this game, even though I have many in my Steam library that I haven't played. Seems like just the right amount of fighting, building and managing to keep things interesting, without being super micro-intensive.
good fun, the campaign is good if u like that sorta thing, but if u just wanna be a merc play careers and punch mechs in the face go for it king
Oh God I don't like this game.  I much prefer the Mechwarrior series.  So WHY do I have like 180 hours on it??!!
I had a 90 percent chance to hit the yes button, idk what happened
Had it for years keep installed to scratch that itch.
Battletech Rocks! So much replay value!
Not the table top but I fricken love it
great game, better with mods.
